title: avenula lodunensis subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Avenula lodunensis subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Avenula pyrenaica or Avenula lodensis, is a subspecies of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of the Pyrenees. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its rich content of essential oils and phenolic compounds. Avenula lodunensis subsp. pyrenaica is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, due to its expectorant and mucolytic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to promote digestive health, soothe coughs, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory ailments.
